sentence: My father is a _____ . He drives a truck.
explanation: DRIVE → driver (add -r to verbs ending in -e to make a person noun)

sentence: She is a good _____ . She teaches English.
explanation: TEACH → teacher (add -er to make a person who teaches)

sentence: He is a famous _____ . He sings beautiful songs.
explanation: SING → singer (add -er to make a person who sings)

sentence: The _____ painted a beautiful picture.
explanation: PAINT → painter (add -er to make a person who paints)

sentence: My mother is a _____ . She works in a bank.
explanation: BANK → banker (add -er to make a person who works in a bank)

sentence: The _____ fixed the broken pipe.
explanation: PLUMB → plumber (add -er to make a person who works with pipes)

sentence: She wants to be a _____ when she grows up. She loves to dance.
explanation: DANCE → dancer (add -r to verbs ending in -e)

sentence: The _____ writes stories for the newspaper.
explanation: WRITE → writer (add -r to verbs ending in -e)

sentence: He is a fast _____ . He runs every morning.
explanation: RUN → runner (double the final consonant and add -er)

sentence: The _____ swims in the pool every day.
explanation: SWIM → swimmer (double the final consonant and add -er)

sentence: My uncle is an _____ . He acts in movies.
explanation: ACT → actor (add -or to make a person who acts)

sentence: The _____ visited our school to check our eyes.
explanation: VISIT → visitor (add -or to make a person who visits)

sentence: She is the _____ of the school. She manages everything.
explanation: DIRECT → director (add -or to make a person who directs)

sentence: The _____ invented a new machine.
explanation: INVENT → inventor (add -or to make a person who invents)

sentence: He is a good _____ . He plays football very well.
explanation: PLAY → player (add -er to make a person who plays)

sentence: The _____ cleaned the office after work.
explanation: CLEAN → cleaner (add -er to make a person who cleans)

sentence: My sister is a _____ . She bakes cakes and bread.
explanation: BAKE → baker (add -r to verbs ending in -e)

sentence: The _____ helped us find our way in the museum.
explanation: GUIDE can be both a verb and a noun — the person who guides is also called a guide.

sentence: He is a _____ . He farms vegetables and fruits.
explanation: FARM → farmer (add -er to make a person who farms)

sentence: The _____ designed a beautiful building.
explanation: DESIGN → designer (add -er to make a person who designs)

sentence: She is a _____ . She reports the news on TV.
explanation: REPORT → reporter (add -er to make a person who reports)

sentence: The _____ helped me carry my bags at the hotel.
explanation: HELP → helper (add -er to make a person who helps)

sentence: My brother is a _____ . He builds houses.
explanation: BUILD → builder (add -er to make a person who builds)

sentence: The _____ collected the tickets at the door.
explanation: COLLECT → collector (add -or to make a person who collects)

sentence: She is a great _____ . She leads the team very well.
explanation: LEAD → leader (add -er to make a person who leads)
